column4: ਧਨਾਸਰੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਆਰਤੀ
column7: Dhanaasaree, First Mehl, Aartee:
column8: Dhanasri 1st Guru. Present adoration.

column4: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ॥
column7: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
column8: There is but One God. By True Guru's grace, He is obtained.

column4: ਗਗਨ ਮੈ ਥਾਲੁ. ਰਵਿ ਚੰਦੁ ਦੀਪਕ ਬਨੇ; ਤਾਰਿਕਾ ਮੰਡਲ ਜਨਕ ਮੋਤੀ॥
column7: In the bowl of the sky, the sun and moon are the lamps; the stars in the constellations are the pearls.
column8: In the sky's salver, the sun and the moon are the lamps and the stars with their orbs, are the studded pearls.

column4: ਧੂਪੁ ਮਲਆਨ ਲੋ. ਪਵਣੁ ਚਵਰੋ ਕਰੇ; ਸਗਲ ਬਨਰਾਇ ਫੂਲੰਤ ਜੋਤੀ॥੧॥
column7: The fragrance of sandalwood is the incense, the wind is the fan, and all the vegetation are flowers in offering to You, O Luminous Lord. ||1||
column8: The fragrance of sandal wood make Thy incense, wind Thy fan and all the vegetation Thine flowers, O Luminous Lord.

column4: ਕੈਸੀ ਆਰਤੀ ਹੋਇ; ਭਵ ਖੰਡਨਾ ਤੇਰੀ ਆਰਤੀ॥
column7: What a beautiful lamp-lit worship service this is! O Destroyer of fear, this is Your Aartee, Your worship service.
column8: What a beautiful worship with lamps is being performed? Such is Thine adoration O Lord, the Destroyer of dread.

column4: ਅਨਹਤਾ ਸਬਦ ਵਾਜੰਤ ਭੇਰੀ॥੧॥ਰਹਾਉ॥
column7: The sound current of the Shabad is the sounding of the temple drums. ||1||Pause||
column8: The celestial strain is the sounding of template drums. Pause.

column4: ਸਹਸ ਤਵ ਨੈਨ. ਨਨ ਨੈਨ ਹੈ ਤੋਹਿ ਕਉ; ਸਹਸ ਮੂਰਤਿ. ਨਨਾ ਏਕ ਤੋਹੀ॥
column7: Thousands are Your eyes, and yet You have no eyes. Thousands are Your forms, and yet You have not even one form.
column8: Thousands are Thine eyes, yet thou hast no eye. Thousands are Thine forms, yet Thou hast not even one.

column4: ਸਹਸ ਪਦ ਬਿਮਲ. ਨਨ ਏਕ ਪਦ; ਗੰਧ ਬਿਨੁ. ਸਹਸ ਤਵ ਗੰਧ; ਇਵ ਚਲਤ ਮੋਹੀ॥੨॥
column7: Thousands are Your lotus feet, and yet You have no feet. Without a nose, thousands are Your noses. I am enchanted with Your play! ||2||
column8: Thousands are Thine holy feet, yet Thou hast not one foot. Thousands are Thine noses and yet Thou art without a nose. I am bewitched by these plays of Thine.

column4: ਸਭ ਮਹਿ ਜੋਤਿ; ਜੋਤਿ ਹੈ ਸੋਇ॥
column7: The Divine Light is within everyone; You are that Light.
column8: Amongst all there is light and that light art Thou.

column4: ਤਿਸ ਕੈ ਚਾਨਣਿ; ਸਭ ਮਹਿ ਚਾਨਣੁ ਹੋਇ॥
column7: Yours is that Light which shines within everyone.
column8: By His Light, the light shines within all the souls.

column4: ਗੁਰ ਸਾਖੀ; ਜੋਤਿ ਪਰਗਟੁ ਹੋਇ॥
column7: By the Guru's Teachings, this Divine Light is revealed.
column8: By Guru's teaching the Divine light becomes manifest.

column4: ਜੋ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ; ਸੁ ਆਰਤੀ ਹੋਇ॥੩॥
column7: That which pleases the Lord is the true worship service. ||3||
column8: Whatever pleases Him, that is His real worship.

column4: ਹਰਿ ਚਰਣਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ. ਲੋਭਿਤ ਮਨੋ; ਅਨਦਿਨੋ ਮੋਹਿ ਆਹੀ ਪਿਆਸਾ॥
column7: My soul is enticed by the honey-sweet lotus feet of the Lord; night and day, I thirst for them.
column8: My soul is bewitched by the honey of the lotus feet of God and, night and day, I am thirsty for them.

column4: ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਜਲੁ ਦੇਹਿ. ਨਾਨਕ ਸਾਰਿੰਗ ਕਉ; ਹੋਇ ਜਾ ਤੇ. ਤੇਰੈ ਨਾਮਿ ਵਾਸਾ॥੪॥੧॥੭॥੯॥
column7: Bless Nanak, the thirsty song-bird, with the water of Your Mercy, that he may come to dwell in Your Name. ||4||1||7||9||
column8: Bless Nanak, the pied cuckoo, with the Nectar of Thine mercy, so that he may have an abode in Thy Name, O Lord.

column4: ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰੋ ਆਰਤੀ ਮਜਨੁ ਮੁਰਾਰੇ॥
column7: Your Name, Lord, is my adoration and cleansing bath.
column8: Thy Name, O Lord, is my adoration in audience and ablution.

column4: ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ; ਝੂਠੇ ਸਗਲ ਪਾਸਾਰੇ॥੧॥ਰਹਾਉ॥
column7: Without the Name of the Lord, all ostentatious displays are useless. ||1||Pause||
column8: Without the Lord's Name, all the ostentations are in vain. Pause.

column4: ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰੋ ਆਸਨੋ. ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰੋ ਉਰਸਾ; ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਕੇਸਰੋ. ਲੇ ਛਿਟਕਾਰੇ॥
column7: Your Name is my prayer mat, and Your Name is the stone to grind the sandalwood. Your Name is the saffron which I take and sprinkle in offering to You.
column8: Thy name is my prayer mat, Thy name, my stone scraper and Thy name the saffron, which I take and sprinkle for Thee.
column9: (ਆਸਨੋ) ਉਪਾਸਨਾ ਸਥਾਨ। (ਉਰਸਾ) ਚੰਦਨ ਰਗੜਨ ਵਾਲਾ ਪੱਥਰ।

column4: ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਅੰਭੁਲਾ. ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰੋ ਚੰਦਨੋ; ਘਸਿ ਜਪੇ. ਨਾਮੁ ਲੇ, ਤੁਝਹਿ ਕਉ ਚਾਰੇ॥੧॥
column7: Your Name is the water, and Your Name is the sandalwood. The chanting of Your Name is the grinding of the sandalwood. I take it and offer all this to You. ||1||
column8: Thy name is the water and Thy Name and sandal. The repetition of Thy Name is the grating of sandal. Taking the Name I offer it unto Thee.

column4: ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਦੀਵਾ. ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰੋ ਬਾਤੀ; ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰੋ ਤੇਲੁ. ਲੇ ਮਾਹਿ ਪਸਾਰੇ॥
column7: Your Name is the lamp, and Your Name is the wick. Your Name is the oil I pour into it.
column8: Thy Name is the Lamp and Thy Name the wick Taking the oil of Thy name, I pour it therein.

column4: ਨਾਮ ਤੇਰੇ ਕੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਲਗਾਈ; ਭਇਓ ਉਜਿਆਰੋ ਭਵਨ ਸਗਲਾਰੇ॥੨॥
column7: Your Name is the light applied to this lamp, which enlightens and illuminates the entire world. ||2||
column8: Thy Name is the light, which I have applied to the lamp and it has enlightened the whole world.

column4: ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰੋ ਤਾਗਾ. ਨਾਮੁ ਫੂਲ ਮਾਲਾ; ਭਾਰ ਅਠਾਰਹ ਸਗਲ ਜੂਠਾਰੇ॥
column7: Your Name is the thread, and Your Name is the garland of flowers. The eighteen loads of vegetation are all too impure to offer to You.
column8: Thy Name is the thread and Thy Name the garland of flowers All the eighteen loads of vegetation are too impure to be offered to Thee.

column4: ਤੇਰੋ ਕੀਆ. ਤੁਝਹਿ ਕਿਆ ਅਰਪਉ; ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰਾ. ਤੁਹੀ ਚਵਰ ਢੋਲਾਰੇ॥੩॥
column7: Why should I offer to You, that which You Yourself created? Your Name is the fan, which I wave over You. ||3||
column8: Why should I offered Thee, What Thou Thyself hast made? Thy Name is the fly-brush, which I wave over Thee.
column9: ਦਾਸਨਦਾਸ ਆਪ ਜੀ ਦਾ (ਕੀਆ) ਪੈਦਾ ਕੀਤਾ ਜੰਤ ਅਤੇ ਆਪ ਜੀ ਦਾ ਪੈਦਾ ਕੀਤਾ ਚੰਦਨ ਆਪ ਜੀ ਕੀ ਅਰਪੇ। (ਢੋਲਾਰੇ) ਝੁਲਾਉਣਾ।

column4: ਦਸ ਅਠਾ ਅਠਸਠੇ ਚਾਰੇ ਖਾਣੀ; ਇਹੈ ਵਰਤਣਿ ਹੈ ਸਗਲ ਸੰਸਾਰੇ॥
column7: The whole world is engrossed in the eighteen Puraanas, the sixty-eight sacred shrines of pilgrimage, and the four sources of creation.
column8: The whole world is engrossed in the eighteen Puranas, sixty-eight shrines and the four sources of creation.

column4: ਕਹੈ ਰਵਿਦਾਸੁ. ਨਾਮੁ ਤੇਰੋ ਆਰਤੀ; ਸਤਿਨਾਮੁ ਹੈ ਹਰਿ ਭੋਗ ਤੁਹਾਰੇ॥੪॥੩॥
column7: Says Ravi Daas, Your Name is my Aartee, my lamp-lit worship-service. The True Name, Sat Naam, is the food which I offer to You. ||4||3||
column8: Says Rav-Dass, Thy name alone is my present worship. The True Name is the food which I offer Thee, O Lord.

column4: ਸ੍ਰੀ ਸੈਣੁ॥
column7: Sri Sain:
column8: Venerable Sain.

column4: ਧੂਪ ਦੀਪ ਘ੍ਰਿਤ ਸਾਜਿ ਆਰਤੀ॥
column7: With incense, lamps and ghee, I offer this lamp-lit worship service.
column8: I offer adoration prayers with incense, lamps and clarified butter.

column4: ਵਾਰਨੇ ਜਾਉ. ਕਮਲਾ ਪਤੀ॥੧॥
column7: I am a sacrifice to the Lord of Lakshmi. ||1||
column8: I am a sacrifice unto the Lord of Lakhshmi.

column4: ਮੰਗਲਾ ਹਰਿ ਮੰਗਲਾ॥
column7: Hail to You, Lord, hail to You!
column8: Hail, to Thee, O God, hail;

column4: ਨਿਤ ਮੰਗਲੁ; ਰਾਜਾਰਾਮ ਰਾਇ ਕੋ॥੧॥ਰਹਾਉ॥
column7: Again and again, hail to You, Lord King, Ruler of all! ||1||Pause||
column8: Eternal bliss is thine, O my Sovereign Lord, the Emperor. Pause.

column4: ਊਤਮੁ ਦੀਅਰਾ; ਨਿਰਮਲ ਬਾਤੀ॥
column7: Sublime is the lamp, and pure is the wick.
column8: The sublime lamp and pure wick art Thou,

column4: ਤੁਹਂੀ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੁ ਕਮਲਾ ਪਾਤੀ॥੨॥
column7: You are immaculate and pure, O Brilliant Lord of Wealth! ||2||
column8: O Bright Lord of wealth.

column4: ਰਾਮਾ ਭਗਤਿ ਰਾਮਾਨੰਦੁ ਜਾਨੈ॥
column7: Raamaanand knows the devotional worship of the Lord.
column8: The Lord's meditation, Rama Nand, my Guru knows.

column4: ਪੂਰਨ ਪਰਮਾਨੰਦੁ ਬਖਾਨੈ॥੩॥
column7: He says that the Lord is all-pervading, the embodiment of supreme joy. ||3||
column8: He describes the Lord as Omnipresent and the Embodiment of Supreme joy.

column4: ਮਦਨ ਮੂਰਤਿ; ਭੈ ਤਾਰਿ ਗੋਬਿੰਦੇ॥
column7: The Lord of the world, of wondrous form, has carried me across the terrifying world-ocean.
column8: The World Master of fascinating form has ferried me across the terrible world ocean.

column4: ਸੈਣੁ ਭਣੈ; ਭਜੁ ਪਰਮਾਨੰਦੇ॥੪॥੨॥
column7: Says Sain, remember the Lord, the embodiment of supreme joy! ||4||2||
column8: Says, Sain, “Remember thou, God, the Embodiment of Supreme joy”.

column4: ਪ੍ਰਭਾਤੀ॥
column7: Prabhaatee:
column8: Parbhati.

column4: ਸੁੰਨ ਸੰਧਿਆ ਤੇਰੀ. ਦੇਵ ਦੇਵਾ ਕਰ; ਅਧਪਤਿ ਆਦਿ ਸਮਾਈ॥
column7: Hear my prayer, Lord; You are the Divine Light of the Divine, the Primal, All-pervading Master.
column8: O God, the Primal and all-pervading Master, thou are the mine of light. Hear Thou, My present adoration.

column4: ਸਿਧ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਅੰਤੁ. ਨਹੀ ਪਾਇਆ; ਲਾਗਿ ਰਹੇ ਸਰਨਾਈ॥੧॥
column7: The Siddhas in Samaadhi have not found Your limits. They hold tight to the Protection of Your Sanctuary. ||1||
column8: The adepts, in their trance have found not Thy limit. They continue to cling o Thy asylum.

column4: ਲੇਹੁ ਆਰਤੀ, ਹੋ ਪੁਰਖ ਨਿਰੰਜਨ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪੂਜਹੁ ਭਾਈ॥
column7: Worship and adoration of the Pure, Primal Lord comes by worshipping the True Guru, O Siblings of Destiny.
column8: O brother, worshiping the True Guru one is blessed with the present adoration of the Immaculate Lord.

column4: ਠਾਢਾ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ. ਨਿਗਮ ਬੀਚਾਰੈ; ਅਲਖੁ. ਨ ਲਖਿਆ ਜਾਈ॥੧॥ਰਹਾਉ॥
column7: Standing at His Door, Brahma studies the Vedas, but he cannot see the Unseen Lord. ||1||Pause||
column8: Standing at His door, Brahma reads the Vedas, but he can see not the Unseeable Lord. Pause.

column4: ਤਤੁ ਤੇਲੁ, ਨਾਮੁ ਕੀਆ ਬਾਤੀ; ਦੀਪਕੁ ਦੇਹ, ਉਜੵਾਰਾ॥
column7: With the oil of knowledge about the essence of reality, and the wick of the Naam, the Name of the Lord, this lamp illluminates my body.
column8: With the oil of truthfulness and the wick of the Lord's Name, I have made the lamp to illumine my body.

column4: ਜੋਤਿ ਲਾਇ ਜਗਦੀਸ ਜਗਾਇਆ; ਬੂਝੈ ਬੂਝਨਹਾਰਾ॥੨॥
column7: I have applied the Light of the Lord of the Universe, and lit this lamp. God the Knower knows. ||2||
column8: Applying the Light of the Lord of the World, I have lit the lamp. The Omniscient Lord alone knows this mystery.

column4: ਪੰਚੇ ਸਬਦ ਅਨਾਹਦ ਬਾਜੇ; ਸੰਗੇ ਸਾਰਿੰਗਪਾਨੀ॥
column7: The Unstruck Melody of the Panch Shabad, the Five Primal Sounds, vibrates and resounds. I dwell with the Lord of the World.
column8: The celestial strain of the five musical instruments resounds within me and I abide ever with the Lord of the world.

column4: ਕਬੀਰ ਦਾਸ. ਤੇਰੀ ਆਰਤੀ ਕੀਨੀ; ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ਨਿਰਬਾਨੀ॥੩॥੫॥
column7: Kabeer, Your slave, performs this Aartee, this lamp-lit worship service for You, O Formless Lord of Nirvaanaa. ||3||5||
column8: O Formless and Detached Lord, Thy slave Kabir, performs such a worship of Thine.

column4: ਧੰਨਾ॥
column7: Dhannaa:
column8: Dhanna.
column9: ਭਗਤ ਧੰਨਾ ਜੀ ਅਕਾਲ ਪੁਰਖ ਜੀ ਤੋਂ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤ ਜੀ ਦੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਲਈ ਲੋੜੀਂਦੀਆਂ ਵਸਤੂਆਂ ਮੰਗਦੇ ਹਨ।

column4: ਗੋਪਾਲ ਤੇਰਾ ਆਰਤਾ॥
column7: O Lord of the world, this is Your lamp-lit worship service.
column8: O Lord, I perform Thine devotional worship.

column4: ਜੋ ਜਨ ਤੁਮਰੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰੰਤੇ; ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਕਾਜ ਸਵਾਰਤਾ॥੧॥ਰਹਾਉ॥
column7: You are the Arranger of the affairs of those humble beings who perform Your devotional worship service. ||1||Pause||
column8: Thou arrangest the affairs of those persons who perform Thine devotional service. Pause.

column4: ਦਾਲਿ ਸੀਧਾ; ਮਾਗਉ ਘੀਉ॥
column7: Lentils, flour and ghee - these things, I beg of You.
column8: I beg of Thee flour and ghee,

column4: ਹਮਰਾ ਖੁਸੀ ਕਰੈ ਨਿਤ ਜੀਉ॥
column7: My mind shall ever be pleased.
column8: so shall my mind be ever pleased.

column4: ਪਨੑੀਆ ਛਾਦਨੁ ਨੀਕਾ॥
column7: Shoes, fine clothes,
column8: The foot-wear, good clothes,

column4: ਅਨਾਜੁ ਮਗਉ; ਸਤ ਸੀਕਾ॥੧॥
column7: And grain of seven kinds - I beg of You. ||1||
column8: and corn of seven sorts, I beg of Thee.

column4: ਗਊ ਭੈਸ ਮਗਉ ਲਾਵੇਰੀ॥
column7: A milk cow, and a water buffalo, I beg of You,
column8: The milch-cow and buffalo I ask for,

column4: ਇਕ ਤਾਜਨਿ ਤੁਰੀ ਚੰਗੇਰੀ॥
column7: And a fine Turkestani horse.
column8: and also a good Turkistani mare.

column4: ਘਰ ਕੀ ਗੀਹਨਿ ਚੰਗੀ॥
column7: A good wife to care for my home
column8: I ask for a good wife to look after my home.
column9: ਦਾਸਨਦਾਸ ਆਪ ਜੀ ਦੇ ਘਰ ਦੀ ਚੰਗੀ (ਗੀਹਨਿ) ਇਸਤ੍ਰੀ ਬਣਕੇ ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰੇ।

column4: ਜਨੁ ਧੰਨਾ ਲੇਵੈ ਮੰਗੀ॥੨॥੪॥
column7: - Your humble servant Dhanna begs for these things, Lord. ||2||4||
column8: Thy slave Dhanna, O Lord, begs Thee to procure them.
